sections.title: Materials
sections.instructions: Match each description to the correct material.

pairs.prompt: Scissors and knives are made of this hard, shiny material.
Windows are made of this transparent material.
Bottles and bags can be made of this light, cheap material.
Scarves and jumpers are made of this warm material from sheep.
Tables and chairs are often made of this material from trees.
Birthday cards and boxes are made of this thick paper.
Books and newspapers are made of this thin, light material.
Crowns and expensive rings are sometimes made of this yellow precious material.
This grey-white precious material is used for jewellery.
Erasers and tyres are made of this stretchy, bouncy material.
This material is very strong. Keys, forks and coins are made of it.
This material grows in forests and is used for furniture.
This material is flexible, waterproof and can stretch.
Mirrors are made with this transparent, smooth material.
Toys and containers are often made of this waterproof material.

sections.title: Properties of Materials
sections.instructions: Match each description to the correct property.

pairs.prompt: This property means something weighs a lot.
This property means something doesn't bend.
This property means something can bend easily.
This property means something doesn't weigh much.
This property means a surface is uneven and not smooth.
This property means a surface is even and nice to touch.
The opposite of heavy.
The opposite of smooth.
The opposite of rigid.
Gold and bricks have this property — they weigh a lot.
Paper and feathers have this property — they don't weigh much.
Rubber and wool have this property — they can bend.
Wood and metal often have this property — they don't bend.
Sandpaper has this surface property.
Glass and polished metal have this surface property.

sections.title: Vocabulary in Context
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: The scarf is made of ___. It's very warm and soft.
Be careful! The vase is made of ___. It can break easily.
She's wearing a ___ orange skirt. The colour is very vivid.
The zebra costume has black and white ___.
The ladybird costume is red with black ___.
My helmet is ___ of gold paper.
This table is made of ___. It came from a tree.
The tyres are made of ___. They are bouncy and flexible.
___ we make costumes for the show?
___ go to the cinema tonight!
___ use blue paper for the sky.
The mask is made of papier-mâché — that's ___, glue and water.
He's ___ a striped shirt and dark trousers.
The brick is very ___. It doesn't bend at all.
This feather is very ___. It weighs almost nothing.
The fairy costume has beautiful ___ on the back.
The crown is made of ___. It's shiny and yellow.
She's wearing a ___ green dress — almost black.
A ___ is a sad or serious show at the theatre.
Sandpaper has a ___ surface.

sections.title: Technology Words
sections.instructions: Match each technology word to its definition.

pairs.prompt: A small portable computer you can carry around.
Small programs you use on a phone or computer.
Wireless internet connection.
A book you read on an electronic screen.
A bag you carry on your back.
A book or website that explains what words mean.
The flat surface you look at on a computer or phone.
A global network that connects computers worldwide.
A page or group of pages you visit online.
To make a duplicate of text or an image.
To place copied text or an image in a new position.
A container for rubbish; also the recycle bin on a computer.
A program you use to visit websites (e.g. Chrome).
To save a file from the Internet to your computer.
A secret word or phrase you use to log in.

sections.title: Map & Direction Words
sections.instructions: Match each map or direction word to its meaning.

pairs.prompt: A map that shows countries and their borders.
A map that shows mountains, rivers and land features.
A map that shows roads and buildings in an area.
Shows how distances on a map relate to real distances.
Explains what the symbols and colours on a map mean.
A diagram showing north, south, east and west.
A small picture on a map representing a real thing.
The line between two countries or regions.
The name that tells you what a map is about.
Continuing forward without turning.
On the other side of the road or area.
Right beside something.
In the middle of two things.
From one side to the other.
Following the length of a road or river.
Going by something without stopping.
Entering one side and coming out the other.
A circular junction where roads meet.
A narrow path for walking.
The most important city of a country.

sections.title: Vocabulary in Context
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: I need to ___ this file from the Internet to my computer.
Can you ___ this text and put it in the other document?
Use a ___ to find information online, for example Google.
You need ___ to connect to the Internet without a cable.
The ___ shows the distance between real places on a map.
A ___ map shows countries and their borders.
Go ___ and then turn left at the traffic lights.
The cinema is ___ the restaurant. It's on the other side of the road.
I read the novel on my ___ during the train journey.
She put all her books in her ___ before leaving for school.
Open your ___ and type the address in the bar.
The ___ on a map explains what each symbol means.
Walk ___ the park and then turn right.
Don't forget to bring your ___ or your phone battery will die.
You ___ listen carefully to your teacher in class.
You ___ talk to your classmates during a test.
She is very good ___ playing the piano.
A ___ map shows mountains, rivers and lakes.
The bank is ___ the supermarket and the post office.
He put the old papers in the ___.
You need to type your ___ to log in to your account.
Take the second turning ___ the left.
I'll ___ the photo into the document.
The compass ___ on a map shows north, south, east and west.
The school is ___ the end of this road.
I use ___ to stick the pictures onto the poster.
She needs ___ to cut out the photos for her project.
Go ___ the tunnel. The station is on the other side.
North is usually at the ___ of a map.
To ___ a file means to send it from your computer to the Internet.

sections.title: Jobs
sections.instructions: Match each description to the correct job.

pairs.prompt: This person serves food and drinks in a restaurant.
This person performs in films, TV shows or on stage.
This person prepares food in a kitchen.
This person drives a car, bus or taxi.
This person writes articles for newspapers or websites.
This person takes photos with a camera.
This person creates paintings, drawings or sculptures.
This person creates the look and style of clothes or objects.
This person sings songs, sometimes in a band or on stage.
This person performs gymnastics and tricks, often in a circus.
This person works on a farm and grows food or keeps animals.
This person brings your food when you eat out.
This person uses a camera at work and takes pictures of people or places.
You have to remember a lot of words for this job and you work on a stage.
This is a good job if you like food and want to work in a kitchen.

sections.title: Personality Adjectives
sections.instructions: Match each description to the correct personality adjective.

pairs.prompt: Someone who is warm and always wants to help other people.
Someone who talks to everyone and makes friends easily.
Someone who learns things quickly and knows a lot.
Someone who tells you exciting stories and you never feel bored.
Someone who doesn't want to work and prefers to do nothing.
Someone who is sweet, cute and very nice.
Someone who is not nice and often hurts other people's feelings.
Someone who doesn't talk to people and is not welcoming.
Someone who everybody knows and likes.
Someone who is not afraid of danger or difficult things.
The opposite of 'kind'.
The opposite of 'friendly'.
The opposite of 'boring'.
The opposite of 'cowardly'.
The opposite of 'hard-working'.

sections.title: Vocabulary in Context
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: The ___ took our order and brought us our drinks.
My uncle is a ___ — he takes beautiful photos of nature.
She wants to be an ___ when she grows up because she loves performing.
The ___ wrote an article about the school play for the newspaper.
He's very ___ — he always gets the best marks in class.
She's very ___ — she never does her homework.
Don Quixote was very ___. He wasn't afraid of anything.
My teacher is very ___ — everyone likes her.
He's very ___ — he doesn't talk to anyone.
My little sister is very ___. She always helps people.
The ___ made a delicious meal for the whole family.
The ___ designed a beautiful new dress for the fashion show.
The ___ performed amazing tricks on the trapeze at the circus.
My grandpa is a ___. He grows strawberries and keeps chickens.
She's very ___ — she tells us fun facts about everything.
The ___ sang a beautiful song at the concert.
The ___ painted a beautiful picture of the sunset.
The baby is so ___! Look at those cute little hands.
The ___ drove the bus carefully through the busy streets.
He's very ___ — he says mean things to other children.
What does your dad ___? He's tall with brown hair.
What ___ your sister like? She's very friendly and kind.
What does your brother ___? He likes playing football.
If you ___ ice, it melts. (zero conditional)
When the sun goes down, it ___ dark. (zero conditional)
If you don't water a plant, it ___. (zero conditional)
The ___ works in a restaurant and takes people's orders.
An ___ studies old objects to learn about the past.
She's got short dark hair and green eyes. That's what she ___.
When water reaches 100°C, it ___. (zero conditional)
